explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: Sources discuss the risks of algorithmic bias and strategies to mitigate it, highlighting the importance of diverse training data. A specific approach using census data to promote diversity in AI-generated content is detailed. The company states a focus on fairness in the application of AI or related business practices.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This Digiday article provides evidence for **fairness**, **governance**, and **oversight** by discussing the risks of algorithmic bias and the strategies agencies are employing to mitigate it. It highlights the importance of diverse training data and the implementation of human-AI oversight, anti-bias units, and advisory services for AI ethics policies to ensure fairness and responsible AI development. The article also details a specific approach using census data to promote diversity in AI-generated content, demonstrating concrete fairness mitigation efforts.
relevant_sources.title: Digiday: "With AI bias still a sticking point for clients, agencies mix human and technical fixes"

relevant_sources.summary: The IPG ESG at Interpublic Group Report (2024) provides evidence for the **governance** and **fairness** pillars of responsible AI. The report's mention of "responsible governance" directly supports the governance pillar, indicating a commitment to ethical oversight. Furthermore, the statement about "equitably" enhancing life suggests a focus on fairness in the application of AI or related business practices.
relevant_sources.title: IPG ESG at Interpublic Group Report (2024)

governance.findings: Sources discuss strategies agencies employ to mitigate algorithmic bias and reference advisory services for AI ethics policies. The 2023 ESG Report describes a platform's function and implies policy-based oversight of AI system capabilities. The IPG ESG at Interpublic Group Report (2024) mentions "responsible governance".
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This Digiday article provides evidence for **fairness**, **governance**, and **oversight** by discussing the risks of algorithmic bias and the strategies agencies are employing to mitigate it. It highlights the importance of diverse training data and the implementation of human-AI oversight, anti-bias units, and advisory services for AI ethics policies to ensure fairness and responsible AI development. The article also details a specific approach using census data to promote diversity in AI-generated content, demonstrating concrete fairness mitigation efforts.
relevant_sources.title: Digiday: "With AI bias still a sticking point for clients, agencies mix human and technical fixes"

relevant_sources.summary: The "2023 ESG Report" provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by describing a platform's function and implying policy-based oversight of AI system capabilities through its integration. The report also supports transparency by detailing mechanisms for employees to report suspected violations, including those related to data and AI systems, via provided contact information.
relevant_sources.title: 2023 ESG Report

relevant_sources.summary: The IPG ESG at Interpublic Group Report (2024) provides evidence for the **governance** and **fairness** pillars of responsible AI. The report's mention of "responsible governance" directly supports the governance pillar, indicating a commitment to ethical oversight. Furthermore, the statement about "equitably" enhancing life suggests a focus on fairness in the application of AI or related business practices.
relevant_sources.title: IPG ESG at Interpublic Group Report (2024)

oversight.findings: Sources reference the implementation of human-AI oversight. The implementation of anti-bias units and advisory services for AI ethics policies is also referenced.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This Digiday article provides evidence for **fairness**, **governance**, and **oversight** by discussing the risks of algorithmic bias and the strategies agencies are employing to mitigate it. It highlights the importance of diverse training data and the implementation of human-AI oversight, anti-bias units, and advisory services for AI ethics policies to ensure fairness and responsible AI development. The article also details a specific approach using census data to promote diversity in AI-generated content, demonstrating concrete fairness mitigation efforts.
relevant_sources.title: Digiday: "With AI bias still a sticking point for clients, agencies mix human and technical fixes"

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: The company discloses its membership in the Partnership on AI (PAI) and announces the formation of an IPG AI Steering Committee. Mechanisms are detailed for employees to report suspected violations related to data and AI systems via provided contact information.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: The "2023 ESG Report" provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by describing a platform's function and implying policy-based oversight of AI system capabilities through its integration. The report also supports transparency by detailing mechanisms for employees to report suspected violations, including those related to data and AI systems, via provided contact information.
relevant_sources.title: 2023 ESG Report

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned report, the "2023 Inclusion + Impact Report," provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It demonstrates this by disclosing IPG and its McCann Worldgroup network's membership in the Partnership on AI (PAI) to address misinformation and biases in AI tools, and by announcing the formation of an IPG AI Steering Committee to foster collective understanding and prioritize equity and responsibility.
relevant_sources.title: 2023 Inclusion + Impact Report

summary.overall_findings: Interpublic Group's public disclosures detail mechanisms for employees to report suspected violations related to data and AI systems, addressing transparency, and discuss strategies agencies employ to mitigate algorithmic bias, addressing governance. These materials address 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, with transparency, fairness, oversight, and governance each supported by documented policies. Further disclosures highlight the importance of diverse training data for fairness and reference the implementation of human-AI oversight. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability, privacy, and 1 additional pillar, based on a review of 14 publicly available sources.
